text: Improvements in neural forecasting have accelerated in recent years as many large-scale forecasting systems have evolved. However, long-horizon forecasting is still an extremely difficult task, and the associated volatility, as well as high computational cost, pose significant obstacles to its adoption. To address these challenges, we propose a novel forecasting model named NHITS (Novel Hierarchical Interpolation Sampling) that utilizes recent hierarchical interpolation and multi-rate sampling techniques. The former compels the model to construct its predictions sequentially focusing on the more important components in the frequency domain and breaking down the input series into smoother sub-sequences to predict a more stable forecast. The latter, inspired by changepoint analysis, leverages different rates at which the input samples are used to generate each prediction of the series. We show theoretically that the hierarchical interpolation scheme enables our model to achieve long-horizon forecasts in the smooth case. Experiment results using several large-scale benchmark datasets from the literature are provided for long-horizon forecasting. Experimental results confirm that our model achieves state-of-the-art results and a gain in average accuracy of almost 20% when compared with other models such as Transformers variants. Meanwhile, computation time is reduced by about an order of magnitude (50 times). Our code can be obtained from https://github.com/Nixtla/neuralforecast.
